name: nuvla-api

metadata.name: nuvla-api
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# Nuvla Python Client Library

[![Build Status](https://github.com/nuvla/python-library/actions/workflows/release.yml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/nuvla/python-library/actions/workflows/release.yml)

Nuvla client library to facilitate interaction with the Nuvla REST API
via the Python language.

## Artifacts

[![PyPi Project](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/nuvla-api?label=nuvla-api)](https://pypi.org/project/nuvla-api)

Starting from **v3.0.0** this library is compatible only with Py3. If you are
still using Py2 (which is not recommended), then install `nuvla-api==2.1.2`.
v2.1.2 will not receive any updates.

## Documentation
You can get the full documentation by typing:

```python
from nuvla.api import Api
help(Api)
```

Or for a specific function:

```python
from nuvla.api import Api
help(Api.search)
```

## Usage

```python
  from nuvla.api import Api
  
  api = Api('https://nuvla.io')
  
  # Login with username & password
  api.login_password('username', 'password')
  # or
  # Login with api-key & secret
  api.login_apikey('credential/uuid', 'secret')

  api.search('user')

  # Logout
  api.logout()
  ```

modules.name: nuvla
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.data.DataObjectS3.create
documentation:
Stores `content` in S3 defined by `s3_cred_id` and registers the
object as data-object in Nuvla. Returns data-object resource ID.
`content` and `content_type` should match (e.g. str and plain/text,
bytes and image/png).

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.data.DataObjectS3.get_content
documentation:
Returns string or bytes array by downloading from S3 the object
identified by `object_id`. They type of the returned object
corresponds to the `content-type` of the object (text or binary).

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.data.DataObjectS3.get_to_file
documentation:
Downloads from S3 and stores to a file the content of the S3 object
identified by `object_id`. If `filename` is not given, the base name
of 'object' attribute is taken instead.

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.data.DataObjectS3.delete
documentation:
Deletes object from S3 and its record from Nuvla identified by
`resource_id`. Returns ID of the deleted object.

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.user.User.create
documentation:
Creates new user according to provided template. Returns new user ID or
throws an exception.
\:param template:
\:return: string

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.user.User.login
documentation:
Logs in the user and returns the Nuvla client.

If the user is logged in using API key.
If the user is logged in using password, the second return value is True.

```
param username_or_key

username or API key

param password_or_secret

password or secret

return

Api
```

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.user.User.login_password
documentation:
Logs in the user using password and returns the Nuvla client.
\:param username: username
\:param password: password
\:return: Api

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.user.User.login_apikey
documentation:
Logs in the user using API key and returns the Nuvla client.
\:param api_key: API key
\:param secret: secret
\:return: Api

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.user.User.switch_group

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.infra_service.InfraServiceK8s.create_from_config
documentation:
Create Kubernetes IS and the corresponding credential from
Kubernetes client `config_fn` file. `group_id` is the ID of the IS group
to add the IS. If `context` is not provided, the 'current-context' from
the `config_fn` is used.

classes.name: nuvla.api.resources.deployment.Deployment

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.deployment.Deployment._set_data
documentation:
---
param deployment: true
param objects: \['\<resource URI>', \]
param records: \['\<resource URI>', \]
param sets: |-
  \[{id: \<resource URI>,

  : filter: '', data-type: '',
    time-start: '', time-end: ''}, \]

  either `id` or `filter` and `data-type` are required.
return: None
---

Content that goes to deployment. One can provide records and/or objects,
where ids and/or filter can be provided.
data:

> records: # optional
>
> : records-ids: \[\] # optional
>   filters: # optional
>
>   > - filter: 'filter'
>   >   data-type: 'data-record'
>   >   time-start: 'timestamp'
>   >   time-stop: 'timestamp'
>
> objects: # optional
>
> : objects-ids: \[\] # optional
>   filters: # optional
>
>   > - filter: 'filter'
>   >   data-type: 'data-object'
>   >   time-start: 'timestamp'
>   >   time-stop: 'timestamp'

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.deployment.Deployment.create
documentation:
Returns deployment as dictionary created from `module_id`.
Sets `infra_cred_id` infrastructure credentials on the deployment, if given.

```
param module_id

str, resource URI

param infra_cred_id

str, resource URI

param data_sets

dict, {id: <resource URI>,

filter: <CIMI filter>,
data-type: data-<record | object>,
time-start: <2020-02-24T13:10:30Z>,
time-end: <2020-02-24T13:11:30Z>}

source:11: (WARNING/2) Definition list ends without a blank line; unexpected unindent.

either  or  and  are required.

param data_records

list of data-record resource URIs

param data_objects

list of data-object resource URIs

return

CimiResource
```

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.deployment.Deployment.get
documentation:
Returns deployment identified by `resource_id` as dictionary.
\:param resource_id: str
\:return: CimiResource

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.deployment.Deployment.terminate
documentation:
Stops and deletes the deployment. Waits at max `timeout` seconds for
the deployment to be fully stoped before attemting deletion.

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.deployment.Deployment.init_logs
documentation:
Creates and returns `deployment-log` resource that corresponds to the
logs of `service` from `deployment` and starting from `since`.
\:param deployment: CimiResource
\:param service: str
\:param since: datetime
\:return: CimiResource

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.deployment.Deployment.get_logs
documentation:
Get `service` logs from `since` till now.
\:param logs: CimiResource
\:return: list: list of lines of the log

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.deployment.Deployment._template_interpolation
documentation:
Returns `string` interpolated by the values from `params`.
Returns an empty string, if either of those is emtpy: `string`, `params`
or the value of the required interpolation key.
Throws ValueError if `params` is missing substitution keys defined in
`string`.

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.deployment.Deployment.get_url
documentation:
Returns interpolated URL defined by `name` in deployment.
Returns an empty string if not all deployment parameters required
for interpolation are filled with values.
Returns None if either URL `name` is not present on the deployment or
interpolation is not possible due to missing deployment parameters. This
means obtaining URL will not be possible.

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.deployment.Deployment.get_parameter
documentation:
Returns value of deployment `resource_id` parameter `name`. To get
global level parameters (not belonging to a node), provide '' or None
as `node_id`.
Returns None if parameter is not found.

methods.name: nuvla.api.resources.deployment.Deployment.get_parameters
documentation:
When `node_id` is not provided, returns dictionary with all
parameters
{'global': {'<param name>': '<param value>', },
 '<node_id>': {'<param name>': '<param value>', },
 ...}
'global' key corresponds to node_id == None or ''.
Returns list with parameters for the provided `node_id`.
Returns empty list or dict if parameters were not found.
Stateless interface to Nuvla module deployment.

modules.name: nuvla.api.api
documentation:
Python wrapper of the Nuvla API.

# Install

## Latest release from pypi

```Bash
$ pip install nuvla-api
```

## Most recent version from source code

```Bash
$ pip install 'https://github.com/nuvla/python-api/archive/main.zip'
```

# Usage

To use this library, import it and instantiate it.
Then use one of the method to login.:

```
from nuvla.api import Api

api = Api()

api.login_password('username', 'password')
```

# Examples

## Login on Nuvla with username and password

```
api.login_password('username', 'password')
```

## Login on Nuvla with key and secret

```
api.login_apikey('credential/ce02ef40-1342-4e68-838d-e1b2a75adb1e',
                 'the-secret-key')
```

# API documentation
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: nuvla.api.api.to_login_params
documentation:
---
param creds: '{''username'': '''', ''password'': ''''} or {''key'': '''', ''secret'':
  ''''}'
return: dict extended with the right 'href' or an empty dict
---

classes.name: nuvla.api.api.SessionStore

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.SessionStore.clear
documentation: Clear cookies for the specified domain.
A `requests.Session` subclass implementing a file-based session store.

classes.name: nuvla.api.api.Api

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.__init__
documentation:
---
param endpoint: Nuvla endpoint (<https://nuvla.io>).
param insecure: Don't check server certificate or you are using a http connection.
---

The http option is strongly unrecommanded.
\:param persist_cookie: Use file to persist cookies.
\:param cookie_file: Allow to specify cookie jar file path.
\:param reauthenticate: Reauthenticate in case of requests failures with status code 401 or 403.
\:param login_creds: {'username': '', 'password': ''} or {'key': '', 'secret': ''}
\:param authn_header: String containing list of claims for authentication header
\:param compress: Compress json data sent to the server. Needs to be supported by the server.

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.login
documentation:
Uses given 'login_params' to log into the Nuvla server. The
'login_params' must be a map containing an "href" element giving the id of
the session-template resource and any other attributes required for the
login method. E.g.:

{"href"

: "username" : "username",
  "password" : "password"}
  or

{"href"

: "key" : "key",
  "secret" : "secret"}

Returns server response as dict. Successful responses will contain a
`status` code of 201 and the `resource-id` of the created session.

```
param   login_params

{"href": "session-template/...", <creds>}

type    login_params

dict

return

Server response.

rtype

requests.Response
```

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.login_internal
documentation:
Deprecated! Use login_password instead. This will be removed in near future.
Login to the server using username and password.

```
param username



param password



return

see login()
```

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.login_password
documentation:
Login to the server using username and password.

```
param username



param password



return

see login()
```

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.login_apikey
documentation:
Login to the server using API key/secret pair.

```
param key

The Key ID (resource id).
(example: credential/ce02ef40-1342-4e68-838d-e1b2a75adb1e)

param secret

The Secret Key corresponding to the Key ID

return

see login()
```

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.logout
documentation: Logout user by deleting his session.

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.current_session
documentation:
Returns current user session or None.

```
return

Current user session.

rtype

str
```

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.get
documentation:
Retreive a CIMI resource by it's resource id

:param      resource_id: The id of the resource to retrieve
:type       resource_id: str

:keyword    select: Select attributes to return. (resource-type always returned)
:type       select: str or list of str

:return:    A CimiResource object corresponding to the resource
:rtype:     CimiResource

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.edit
documentation:
Edit a CIMI resource by it's resource id

:param      resource_id: The id of the resource to edit
:type       resource_id: str

:param      data: The data to serialize into JSON
:type       data: dict

:keyword    select: Cimi select parameter, used to delete an existing attribute from a cimi resource when the
            selected fields are not present in the data argument (e.g description, value)
:type       select: str or list of str

:return:    A CimiResource object
:rtype:     CimiResource

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.edit_patch
documentation:
Edit a CIMI resource by it's resource id with JSON Patch format (RFC 6902)

:param      resource_id: The id of the resource to edit
:type       resource_id: str

:param      data: List of edit patches to apply (in JSON Patch format)
:type       data: list

:keyword    select: Cimi select parameter, used to delete an existing attribute from a cimi resource when the
            selected fields are not present in the data argument (e.g description, value)
:type       select: str or list of str

:return:    A CimiResource object
:rtype:     CimiResource

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.delete
documentation:
Delete a CIMI resource by it's resource id

:param  resource_id: The id of the resource to delete
:type   resource_id: str

:return:    A CimiResponse object which should contain the attributes 'status', 'resource-id' and 'message'
:rtype:     CimiResponse

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.delete_bulk
documentation:
Bulk delete CIMI resources of the given type (Collection).

:param      resource_type: Type of the resource (Collection name).
:type       resource_type: str

:param      Non-empty CIMI filter.
:type       filter: str

:return:    A CimiResponse object with the number of deleted records and other statistics.
:rtype:     CimiResponse

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.operation_bulk
documentation:
Bulk delete CIMI resources of the given type (Collection).

:param      resource_type: Type of the resource (Collection name).
:type       resource_type: str

:param      Non-empty CIMI filter.
:type       filter: str

:param      data: The data to serialize into JSON
:type       data: dict

:return:    A CimiResponse object which should contain the attributes 'status', 'resource-id', 'message' and 'location'
:rtype:     CimiResponse

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.add
documentation:
Add a CIMI resource to the specified resource_type (Collection)

:param      resource_type: Type of the resource (Collection name)
:type       resource_type: str

:param      data: The data to serialize into JSON
:type       data: dict

:return:    A CimiResponse object which should contain the attributes 'status', 'resource-id' and 'message'
:rtype:     CimiResponse

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.search
documentation:
Search for CIMI resources of the given type (Collection).

:param      resource_type: Type of the resource (Collection name)
:type       resource_type: str

:keyword    first: Start from the 'first' element (1-based)
:type       first: int

:keyword    last: Stop at the 'last' element (1-based)
:type       last: int

:keyword    filter: CIMI filter
:type       filter: str

:keyword    select: Select attributes to return. (resource-type always returned)
:type       select: str or list of str

:keyword    expand: Expand linked resources (not implemented yet)
:type       expand: str or list of str

:keyword    orderby: Sort by the specified attribute
:type       orderby: str or list of str

:keyword    aggregation: CIMI aggregation
:type       aggregation: str (operator:field)

:return:    A CimiCollection object with the list of found resources available
            as a generator with the method 'resources()' or with the attribute 'resources_list'
:rtype:     CimiCollection

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.operation
documentation:
Execute an operation on a CIMI resource

:param      resource: The resource to execute operation on.
:type       resource: CimiResource

:param      operation: Operation name (e.g. describe)
:type       operation: str

:param      data: The data to serialize into JSON
:type       data: dict

:return:    A CimiResponse object which should contain the attributes 'status', 'resource-id', 'message' and 'location'
:rtype:     CimiResponse

methods.name: nuvla.api.api.Api.hook
documentation:
Execute a hook operation

:param      operation: Operation name
:type       operation: str

:param      data: The data to serialize into JSON
:type       data: dict

:return:    A CimiResponse object which should contain the attributes 'status', 'resource-id', 'message' and 'location'
:rtype:     CimiResponse
This class is a Python wrapper&helper of the native Nuvla REST API
